api/spec/packages/legacy/src/entitlement.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the v2 entitlements and grants API surface under /api/v2/. Composes V1 entitlement/grant models via OmitProperties spreads to expose customer-scoped CRUD, value/history/reset sub-routes, and admin list endpoints — all compiled into api/openapi.yaml Go server stubs and SDKs.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for polymorphic entitlement types
patterns.description: Union types use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) with a literal `type` field on each variant. Both response (EntitlementV2) and input (EntitlementV2CreateInputs) unions follow this shape.
patterns.example:
@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })
union EntitlementV2 { metered: EntitlementMeteredV2, static: EntitlementStaticV2, boolean: EntitlementBooleanV2 }

patterns.name: OmitProperties for model composition
patterns.description: V2 models reuse V1 models via OmitProperties<Base, "field1" | "field2"> spreads instead of copy-pasting fields. Deprecated fields are kept on create-input models with #deprecated annotations.

patterns.name: Interface-per-route-group with @route + @tag
patterns.description: Each distinct URL prefix gets its own interface decorated with @route, @tag, and @friendlyName. Sub-routes use @route on individual operations. Never mix URL prefixes inside one interface.

patterns.name: Explicit operationId with V2 suffix
patterns.description: Every operation carries @operationId("verbNounV2") in camelCase with a V2 suffix to produce stable SDK method names distinct from V1 counterparts.

patterns.name: PaginatedResponse with QueryPagination/QueryOrdering spreads
patterns.description: All list operations return PaginatedResponse<T> and spread ...QueryPagination, ...QueryLimitOffset, and ...QueryOrdering<OrderByEnum> for consistent pagination params.

patterns.name: V2 suffix on all exported symbols
patterns.description: Every model, union, and interface name exported from this package ends with V2 (e.g. EntitlementV2, GrantV2, GrantCreateInputV2) to avoid SDK symbol collisions with V1.
patterns.example: model GrantV2 { ... }  // NOT Grant or EntitlementGrant

patterns.name: CommonErrors + typed error union on every operation
patterns.description: Every operation returns | CommonErrors at minimum, plus | NotFoundError, | ConflictError where applicable. Never omit error unions.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point: imports all sibling .tsp files and the parent namespace. Declares no models — only wires the package together.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new .tsp files added to this folder must be imported here; forgetting breaks compilation silently.

key_file_guides.role: Defines admin list/get endpoints at /api/v2/entitlements and the canonical discriminated union models (EntitlementV2, EntitlementV2CreateInputs) plus all three variant models (Metered, Boolean, Static).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new entitlement sub-type requires updating both EntitlementV2 and EntitlementV2CreateInputs union definitions here.

key_file_guides.role: Customer-scoped CRUD plus value/history/reset/grants sub-routes split across two interfaces for two URL prefixes. Defines EntitlementValueV2 response model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Two separate interfaces for the two URL prefixes; sub-resource routes (/grants, /value, /history, /reset) belong in CustomerEntitlementV2Endpoints (the second interface), not the first.

key_file_guides.role: Pure model file defining GrantV2 response model and GrantCreateInputV2 create-input model. No HTTP routes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Does NOT use `using TypeSpec.Http` — adding HTTP decorators here requires adding the import and using declaration first.

key_file_guides.role: Admin list endpoint at /api/v2/grants. Intentionally thin — no per-grant routes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses both QueryPagination and QueryLimitOffset spread params; all new list endpoints in this package must do the same.

anti_patterns: Redefining V1 models instead of composing via OmitProperties — creates divergence when V1 types change.
Omitting the V2 suffix from model, union, or interface friendly names — causes SDK name collisions with V1 symbols.
Adding a new entitlement type variant without updating both EntitlementV2 and EntitlementV2CreateInputs discriminated unions in entitlements.tsp.
Forgetting to import a new .tsp file in main.tsp — the file compiles in isolation but is invisible to the package.
Using HTTP decorators in a file that lacks `import "@typespec/http"` and `using TypeSpec.Http` (e.g. grant.tsp) — causes compilation failure.

decisions.decision: V2 models compose V1 types via OmitProperties rather than standalone redefinitions
decisions.rationale: Keeps V1→V2 delta minimal and auditable; V1 field changes propagate automatically unless explicitly omitted.

decisions.decision: Deprecated fields (issueAfterReset, issueAfterResetPriority) kept on create-input with #deprecated instead of removed
decisions.rationale: SDK consumers using V1-style fields must not break on upgrade; the V2 replacement (issue: IssueAfterReset) is additive.

decisions.decision: Two separate interfaces in customer.tsp for the two /customers/{id}/entitlements URL prefixes
decisions.rationale: TypeSpec interface-level @route applies to all operations; splitting ensures sub-resource paths (/grants, /value, /history, /reset) don't pollute the top-level collection interface.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new list filter param to an existing V2 list endpoint
